description: Dropdown selector with math notation rendering, bound to a variable. Uses display() for label, type() for value type, and ref() for default selection index. Produces an assignment.
utility: Create mathematical dropdown selector with LaTeX rendering

arguments.name: position
arguments.description: Position on canvas where dropdown appears

arguments.name: variableName
arguments.description: Quoted variable name to bind the dropdown to

arguments.name: values
arguments.description: value() expression containing list of numeric or symbolic options

arguments.name: display
arguments.description: Label displayed next to dropdown using display() expression

arguments.name: type
arguments.description: Value type: number for numeric values, math for symbolic/expression values

arguments.name: ref
arguments.description: 1-based index of the default selected value

arguments.name: styles
arguments.description: Optional styling such as c(color), fs(fontSize), etc.

alternates.syntax: x = dropdown(at(3, 4), "x", value(1, 2, 3), display("x"), type(number), ref(1))
dd_eq1 = dropdown(at(2, 44), "eq1", value("a*x^2 + b*x + c", "a*sin(b*x)"), display("f(x)"), type(math), ref(1))

examples.expression: dd_eq1 = dropdown(at(2, 44), "eq1", value("a*x^2 + b*x + c", "a*sin(b*x)", "a*exp(-b*x^2)"), display("f(x)"), type(math), ref(2))
mathexpr_1 = dropdown(at(14, 37), "x", value(1, 2), display("x"), type(number), ref(1))
dropdown_1 = dropdown(at(11, 37), "x0", value("x", "x^2", "sin(x)"), display("x0"), type(math), ref(1))
